# Regime scoring comparison (issues #2, #50)

Backtest period: **1980-01-01 -> 2024-12-31 (45.0y)**. Rebalance: quarterly. Costs: default schedule.

## Results

| Strategy | CAGR | Vol | Sharpe | Max DD | Calmar | Avg Turnover |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| AllWeather | 8.28% | 8.59% | 0.96 | -22.9% | 0.36 | 0.030 |
| BigCycle binary | 8.09% | 9.72% | 0.83 | -32.4% | 0.25 | 0.055 |
| BigCycle scored | 7.84% | 9.35% | 0.84 | -31.3% | 0.25 | 0.064 |
| BigCycle non-sovereign | 7.50% | 19.10% | 0.39 | -61.1% | 0.12 | 0.068 |

## Per-decade breakdown

CAGR and max drawdown within each calendar decade (portfolio value reset
at decade start for CAGR; drawdown computed on the decade slice). Useful
for separating inflationary episodes (partial 1970s tail, 2020s) from
disinflation episodes (1980s-2010s).

| Decade | AllWeather CAGR | AllWeather Max DD | BigCycle binary CAGR | BigCycle binary Max DD | BigCycle scored CAGR | BigCycle scored Max DD | BigCycle non-sovereign CAGR | BigCycle non-sovereign Max DD |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1980s | 12.33% | -13.8% | 10.24% | -11.9% | 10.69% | -12.3% | 8.32% | -10.3% |
| 1990s | 9.25% | -7.4% | 8.46% | -5.7% | 7.43% | -6.0% | 6.16% | -15.1% |
| 2000s | 6.44% | -16.7% | 7.50% | -25.9% | 7.69% | -23.7% | 9.21% | -34.4% |
| 2010s | 7.17% | -9.6% | 7.05% | -13.1% | 6.57% | -11.6% | 5.22% | -24.9% |
| 2020s | 4.59% | -22.9% | 6.52% | -32.4% | 6.09% | -31.3% | 9.90% | -61.1% |

## Interpretation

Over 1980-01-01 -> 2024-12-31 (45.0y), the BigCycle variants posted Sharpes of 0.83 (binary), 0.84 (scored), and 0.39 (non-sovereign-heavy base). AllWeather came in at 0.96. Max drawdowns: -32.4% (binary), -31.3% (scored), -61.1% (non-sovereign), -22.9% (AllWeather). The non-sovereign drawdown gap to AllWeather is -38.1% (scored gap was -8.3%). Non-sovereign vs. scored on max DD: -29.8% (positive = shallower; negative = deeper).

**Scale caveat:** 1980-2024 is cyclical/secular scale only. This backtest is silent on transition-scale claims per `specs/theses/changing-world-order/backtest-sample-scope.md` — the US-ascendant, reserve-currency-intact period doesn't sample empire-decline or monetary-regime-transition dynamics. **AllWeather is not a neutral benchmark** per `specs/theses/changing-world-order/bond-allocation.md`: its 40% long-bond core is itself a thesis, and the 1980-2024 disinflation tailwind favored bond-heavy allocations broadly. 'Beating AllWeather' is not the success criterion here; the question is whether reducing sovereign-liability exposure in the base changes cyclical behavior in thesis-consistent ways (specifically: inflationary decades).

The honest read: this test isolates whether base-weight choice (not
regime classifier sophistication) is the lever that drives the drawdown
gap between BigCycle and AllWeather. Pull the sovereign-liability share
from 45% to 25% and see which metrics move. A shallower drawdown in the
2020s decade row (the only clean inflationary episode in the sample) is
the most thesis-aligned outcome; a shallower *total-period* drawdown
would be stronger cyclical-scale evidence; unchanged or worse metrics
would push the thesis back toward 'directionally right but insufficient'
or falsification at cyclical scale.

## Method

- `AllWeather`: Bridgewater-style fixed mix (30/40/15/7.5/7.5).
- `BigCycle binary`: original discrete regime classifier — hard thresholds
  on yield curve, inflation, and real rate pick one of four regimes and
  apply the full corresponding nudge from `REGIME_NUDGES`.
- `BigCycle scored`: consumes `src.indicators.regime_classifier()` to
  produce a continuous score in `[0, 1]` per regime, then blends the
  per-regime nudges weighted by those scores.
- `BigCycle non-sovereign`: identical regime-scored logic, but with the
  non-sovereign-heavy base profile: 30% equities / 5% long bonds / 5%
  short bonds / 25% gold / 20% commodities / 15% cash (sovereign-
  liability share drops from 45% to 25%). See issue #50 and
  `specs/theses/changing-world-order/bond-allocation.md`.
